sourceMovie.title: Run
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Run for the same reason it broke through in 2020: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a horror rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Aneesh Chaganty keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The thriller and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Don't Move
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Run clicked for you, Don't Move (2024) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Bitter Moon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Run clicked for you, Bitter Moon (1992) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Run?
faq.answer: Try Don't Move, Bitter Moon, Sightless—each shares DNA with Run while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Run?
faq.answer: Run (2020) is best described as Thriller, Horror, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Run worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
